# Cost Forecast — 2026-04-25

**Subscription:** Azure subscription 1 (`ae5e40cd-96ef-48d8-950a-2e22cf8f991a`)
**Currency:** GBP
**Baseline window:** 2026-04-21 → 2026-04-24 (4 full days post-optimization)
**Generated by:** /cost-forecast

---

## Headline

| Horizon          | Bottom-up forecast | Azure forecast | Delta    | Confidence |
|------------------|-------------------:|---------------:|---------:|------------|
| Rest of month    | £5.12              | £0.54          | +£4.58   | High       |
| Next 30 days     | £30.74             | £58.90         | −£28.16  | Medium     |
| Next quarter     | £95.00             | ~£176.00       | −£81.00  | Low        |

**TL;DR:** Spend has collapsed from a peak of £7.65/day (2026-04-13) to ~£1.02/day after a sequence of telemetry optimisations that landed between 2026-04-18 and 2026-04-21. Azure's forecast is wrong in two opposite directions: it under-forecasts the rest of April (it anchors to the very low last-3-day average, missing the real £1/day run-rate) and massively over-forecasts May (it pattern-matches to April's mid-month spike, which was a one-time infrastructure ramp-up, not a recurring pattern).

---

## Why Azure's forecast is wrong

- **Rest of month (April 26–30) — under-forecast by £4.58.** Azure sees today (2026-04-25) at £0.41 (partial day) and projects ~£0.107/day for the remaining five days. The actual post-optimisation run-rate (2026-04-21–24 full days, excl. today) is £1.02/day, driven by Cosmos DB serverless RU consumption (~£0.55/day) and ACR storage overage (~£0.13/day). Azure's model anchored to an unusually quiet partial day and under-shot by ~10×.

- **May 2026 — over-forecast by ~£28.** Azure's forecast API projects a spending spike in mid-May (2026-05-12–16: £5–7.5/day) that mirrors April's peak. April's peak was caused by high log ingestion before the DCR+Basic-Logs changes (PRs #254, #255, #273) and App Insights churn from polled telemetry before the Cosmos span filter (PR #238). None of those conditions will recur — the workspace daily cap is set to 1 GB/day, AppTraces and ContainerAppConsoleLogs are on the Basic Logs plan, and the console provider was dropped. Log Analytics MTD spend for April 21–24 is £0.0014 (four days, near-zero). Azure's forecast inherits the April spike as a recurring pattern; it is not.

- **April 11 step-change (£0.66 → £5.08/day).** This is what Azure's pattern-matching is replicating for May. The root cause was: the first full prod Container App deployment (PR #217, 2026-04-11) generated ~1.5 GB/day of AppDependencies (Cosmos 2xx spans) + ~0.3 GB/day of ContainerAppConsoleLogs duplicated through the legacy `log-analytics` destination. Actuals confirmed the cost increase on that date and the subsequent gradual decline as optimisations landed.

- **Verified change-points in the actuals timeline:**

  | Date       | PR      | Change                                          | Before    | After     |
  |------------|---------|--------------------------------------------------|-----------|-----------|
  | 2026-04-18 | #254    | DCR+Basic plan, drop console provider            | £5.1/day  | £2.4/day  |
  | 2026-04-18 | #252    | Delete App Insights availability tests           | (above)   | (above)   |
  | 2026-04-21 | #273    | AppTraces table → Basic Logs                     | £3.1/day  | £0.77/day |

  Note: PRs #237 (2026-04-15, reduce retention to 14 days) was **immediately reverted** by PRs #239 and #240 (same day) because PerGB2018 and App Insights both have a 30-day minimum. No cost impact was ever visible in actuals. Similarly, PR #294 (2026-04-24, ACR retention policy) was reverted by PR #295 (same day) because Basic ACR SKU rejects the RetentionPolicy block — the only effect was a Cosmos deployment-day spike on 2026-04-24 (£1.53 vs typical £0.40–0.55/day).

_Run-rate = mean daily cost 2026-04-21 through 2026-04-24 (4 full post-optimisation days). April 24 included despite being a deployment day — the spike is real and recurs with every deploy._

|-------------------------------|----------------------|---------------:|-------------------:|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|

| acrtowncriershared            | Container Registry   | £0.133         | £0.67              | 15.95 GB / 10 GB Basic free tier (148.5%). Overage is main driver.    |

| job-tc-poll-safety-net-prod   | Azure Container Apps | £0.010         | £0.05              | Legacy name; superceded by job-tc-poll-bootstrap-prod from Apr 22.    |
| job-tc-digest-hourly-prod     | Azure Container Apps | £0.005         | £0.02              | Hourly cron.                                                          |
| job-tc-digest-hourly-dev      | Azure Container Apps | £0.004         | £0.02              | Hourly cron.                                                          |
| job-tc-poll-bootstrap-prod    | Azure Container Apps | £0.003         | £0.01              | Schedule every 30 min; no-op when queue is healthy.                   |
| log-town-crier-shared         | Log Analytics        | £0.001         | £0.01              | Near-zero post-DCR+Basic-Logs. 1 GB/day cap active. LOW CONFIDENCE.  |
| appi-town-crier-shared        | Azure Monitor        | £0.000         | £0.00              | Cost via workspace billing (Log Analytics). Effectively zero.         |
| acs-town-crier-shared         | Email                | £0.001         | £0.01              | Minimal email send cost.                                              |
| sb-tc-prod (Service Bus)      | Service Bus          | £0.000         | £0.00              | Basic tier; essentially free.                                         |
| **Total**                     |                      | **£1.024**     | **£5.12**          |                                                                       |

_LOW CONFIDENCE flag on Log Analytics: only 4 days of post-optimization history. If ingestion is above zero on some days (e.g. AppRequests or AppDependencies for prod traffic), this may tick upward._

---

## Recommendations

### 1. Purge untagged ACR images to bring `acrtowncriershared` under 10 GB

- **Estimated saving:** ~£1.50–2.00/month
- **Bead:** `tc-vtgx`
- **Rationale:** `acrtowncriershared` is a Basic SKU ACR. The Basic tier is consumption-only (no monthly fee); storage is billed per GB at the empirical rate of ~£0.0083/GB/day implied by the actuals. Current usage is 15.95 GB (148.5% of the nominal 10 GB free tier — though "free" is a misnomer; Basic ACR has no actual free-storage tier, the 10 GB is just what's cited in the docs). Reducing to ~8 GB would cut cost from £0.133/day → ~£0.067/day, a saving of ~£1.98/month. The revision cap (`maxInactiveRevisions=5`) was deployed in PR #294 (2026-04-24) and will prevent future accumulation, but existing untagged manifests must be purged manually via `az acr purge`. Note: the `RetentionPolicy` that would automate this was reverted in PR #295 — Basic ACR does not support it.

### 2. Investigate App* table retention discrepancy (90 days vs workspace 30 days)

- **Estimated saving:** £0 currently; up to ~£3–5/month if 90-day retention is billed as data grows
- **Bead:** `tc-23yb`
- **Rationale:** The Table API shows `AppRequests`, `AppDependencies`, `AppExceptions`, `AppMetrics`, and `AppPageViews` with `ret=90` days, while the Log Analytics workspace is configured at 30 days (PR #240, 2026-04-15) and App Insights `RetentionInDays = 30` is set in Pulumi. Under PerGB2018, data stored beyond 31 days incurs archival charges (~£0.019/GB/month). The discrepancy may reflect stale API metadata rather than actual billing, but it should be verified. With near-zero current ingestion the exposure is minimal today; once prod receives real traffic and these tables fill, the gap could become meaningful.

---

## Methodology footnote

- **Actuals data:** Cost Management query API, 60-day window (2026-02-24 → 2026-04-25), daily granularity, grouped by `ResourceId` and `ServiceName`. No pagination required (all results in first page).
- **Baseline window:** 2026-04-21 → 2026-04-24 (4 days). Chosen as the first four complete days after the AppTraces → Basic Logs change (PR #273, 2026-04-21) which was the final step in the optimisation sequence. April 24 included despite being a deployment day to avoid understating deployment-related Cosmos churn, which is a real and recurring cost.
- **Azure forecast source:** Cost Management forecast API, Custom timeframe 2026-04-25 → 2026-05-24.
- **Pulumi reconciliation:** Export unavailable — `PULUMI_ACCESS_TOKEN` not present in non-interactive mode. Azure resource list used as ground truth. No drift or orphaned resources identified via the resource list and job enumeration.
- **Exclusions:** `acrtowncrierdev` (£0.023/60d) and `acrtowncrierprod` (£0.008/60d) excluded from bottom-up table — cost is immaterial. Old job names (`job-town-crier-poll-*`, `job-tc-poll-dev`, `job-tc-poll-safety-net-prod`) appear in MTD actuals from before the April renames/deletes; excluded from forward projection.
- **Growth assumption:** Next-quarter forecast assumes zero user growth (£1.024/day flat). Cosmos is serverless — real-user traffic will increase this line. No adjustment made; call it out as the main uncertainty.
